query: Port of Baltimore Carnival Cruise Line contract extension 2025 year-round service through 2030

{"query":"Port of Baltimore Carnival Cruise Line contract extension 2025 year-round service through 2030","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1643,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://governor.maryland.gov/news/press-releases/governor-moore-applauds-new-five-year-agreement-between-carnival-cruise-line-and-port-baltimore","title":"Governor Moore Applauds New Five-Year Agreement Between Carnival Cruise Line and Port of Baltimore | The Office of Governor Wes Moore","content":"ANNAPOLIS, MD – Governor Wes Moore today applauded a new contract with Carnival Cruise Line that keeps the world’s largest cruise provider serving the Helen Delich Bentley Port of Baltimore for another five years.
The agreement takes effect on January 1, 2025 and includes a five-year renewal option.
“We’re very happy to continue our relationship with Carnival for at least another five years,” said Maryland Port Administration Executive Director Jonathan Daniels.
The Port of Baltimore’s cruise terminal is 2.5 miles from Baltimore’s world-famous Inner Harbor and 10 miles from BWI Thurgood Marshall Airport.
The Baltimore-Washington and DC-Northern Virginia consumer market is also one of the largest and most affluent in the nation."},{"url":"https://content.govdelivery.com/accounts/MDMPA/bulletins/40831db","title":"MARYLAND PORT ADMINISTRATION APPLAUDS ARRIVAL OF CARNIVAL MIRACLE AT PORT OF BALTIMORE","content":"MDOT Maryland Port Administration sent this bulletin at 02/06/2026 03:59 PM EST
Last year, the Carnival Pride began operating under a new five-year contract with the MPA, sailing year-round from the Port of Baltimore to the Bahamas, Bermuda, Caribbean, and Greenland/Canada.
This service is provided to you at no charge by Maryland Port Administration."},{"url":"https://www.wbaltv.com/article/maryland-5-year-deal-carnival-cruise-line-port-of-baltimore/62923013","title":"Maryland signs new deal to keep Carnival cruises in Baltimore","content":"Maryland signed a new five-year contract with Carnival Cruise Line to stay in Baltimore, the governor's office announced Friday.The Baltimore-based Carnival Pride was the first year-round cruise ship to dock at the Helen Delich Bentley Port of Baltimore in 2009."The international cruise industry recognizes Baltimore as one of the top cruise markets in our nation.
"Carnival is a longtime partner and supporter of the Port of Baltimore and we are very happy to continue that relationship."This is the 20th year that Carnival has been cruising from Baltimore, and the new agreement takes effect in January."We're truly proud of our long history of sailing from Baltimore, and grateful for the strong partnership with the port and its great city and state, as we get ready to commemorate 20 years of service here in October," Carnival Cruise Line President Christine Duffy said in a statement.
Carnival currently offers five- to 14-day cruises from Baltimore to destinations that include the Bahamas, Bermuda and other Caribbean locations, as well as New England and Canada."},{"url":"https://careersourcenortheastflorida.com/jaxport-board-approves-contract-extension-with-carnival/","title":"JaxPort board approves contract extension with Carnival - CareerSource NEFL","content":"The revised agreement with Carnival Cruise Line will keep the company in Jacksonville through 2026 and includes renewal options that extend through 2030.
6 approval of a deal with Norwegian Cruise Line to operate its Norwegian Gem cruise ship from JaxPort beginning in November 2025.
Eric Green, JaxPort CEO, said the contract extension was a product of ongoing conversations between the port and Carnival.
JaxPort’s agreement with Norwegian, which extends through 2028, calls for Norwegian to pay an annual guarantee of $1.61 million, for a total of $4.85 million over the contract term.
The cruise industry supports nearly 800 jobs and more than $187 million in annual economic impact for Northeast Florida."}]}
